title: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: This core course provides in-depth study of fundamental data structures and the algorithms that operate on them. Students learn to analyze algorithm efficiency using Big-O notation and select appropriate data structures for specific applications. Topics include arrays, linked lists, trees (binary, AVL, B-trees), heaps, hash tables, and graphs. Algorithm topics include sorting (mergesort, quicksort, heapsort), searching, graph traversal (BFS, DFS), shortest path algorithms, and introduction to dynamic programming. Students implement major data structures from scratch in Java/C++ and analyze their time and space complexity. Prerequisites include CS102 (for OOP proficiency) and MATH120 (for mathematical maturity needed for algorithm analysis). Weekly problem sets combine implementation with theoretical analysis.
topics: Stacks and queues (array and linked implementations)
Binary trees and binary search trees
Balanced trees: AVL trees and Red-Black trees
outcomes: Analyze algorithm time and space complexity using Big-O notation
Implement and use fundamental data structures
Select appropriate data structures for given problems
Implement and compare sorting and searching algorithms
Apply graph algorithms to solve connectivity and path problems
Prove correctness of simple algorithms using loop invariants
